successCriteria.description: src/modules/metrics/metrics.model.ts exists and exports MetricsResponse with fields { requests: number; uptime: number }
src/modules/metrics/metrics.repository.interface.ts exists and exports IMetricsRepository with methods getRequestCount, incrementRequestCount, getUptimeSeconds, resetCounters
src/modules/metrics/metrics.repository.ts exists and exports MetricsRepository implementing IMetricsRepository, using the pool instance from src/shared/db/connection.ts
src/modules/metrics/metrics.service.interface.ts exists and exports IMetricsService with methods getMetrics and resetMetrics returning Promise<MetricsResponse>
src/modules/metrics/migrations/001_create_metrics_table.sql exists with the canonical metrics table schema and seed INSERT for request_count=0
All TypeScript files compile without errors (npm run build passes)

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through IMetricsRepository interface — MetricsRepository is the only file that touches the database
No direct DB access outside repository: MetricsRepository is the sole data-access layer for the metrics module
No sensitive data in logs: Metrics counters (request_count, uptime) are non-sensitive but must still avoid logging any PII or tokens
Error handling: All async repository methods must catch and handle errors — no unhandled promise rejections
No imports from other modules: The metrics module must not depend on leave, balance, employee, policy, or notification modules
Dependency direction: Metrics module depends only on src/shared/db/connection.ts (shared infrastructure); no reverse dependencies permitted
No @gestalt/* packages: Do not add any @gestalt/* internal platform packages as dependencies
outOfScope: Phase 3: RBAC hooks and middleware for the metrics endpoint — deferred
Phase 3: Plugin registration wiring into the Fastify app — deferred
The GET /metrics HTTP endpoint itself — not delivered in this phase
Unit tests for the repository or service — not in scope for this foundation phase
